failure_surface: Output file failed evaluation test despite successful forward fill completion. The solution rewrote the entire worksheet via pandas.to_excel(), which may have altered structure, dimensions, or formatting outside the specified target range (B2:B30). Additionally, pandas Series.fillna(method='ffill') uses deprecated syntax.
root_cause: When manipulating a specific cell range indicated by answer_position, using pandas' ExcelWriter with to_excel() completely rewrites the sheet, potentially changing row/column counts, losing existing styles, and affecting cells outside the intended manipulation scope. The agent did not validate that modifications were confined to the specified range or preserve surrounding content.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: For Cell-Level Manipulation tasks with defined target ranges, read the original workbook entirely, perform the manipulation using openpyxl directly on the specific cell range, then save without rewriting the entire worksheet structure. Validate that only cells within answer_position were modified.
evidence: Test case result was 0 (failure) despite Null count after fill showing NewColumn: 0 (operation appeared complete)
Solution used pandas.ExcelWriter with df.to_excel(), which rewrites entire worksheet rather than modifying in-place
Warning message: 'FutureWarning: Series.fillna with method is deprecated and will raise in future version'
Task specified Cell-Level Manipulation with answer_position B2:B30, but no validation confirmed only that range was modified
No check existed for whether original structure/styling outside target range was preserved

memory_items.title: Range-Constrained Cell-Level Editing
memory_items.lesson: When a task specifies a precise target cell range for manipulation, use direct cell-by-cell editing rather than bulk DataFrame operations that rewrite the entire sheet structure.
memory_items.generalization: For cell-level edits to specific ranges, prefer openpyxl's direct cell manipulation (ws.cell().value = ...) over pandas.to_excel() to ensure only the designated range is affected and surrounding structure remains unchanged.

memory_items.title: Validation of Scope-Limited Operations
memory_items.lesson: After completing manipulations on a specified cell range, verify that no unintended changes occurred outside the target area.
memory_items.generalization: Implement post-operation validation comparing pre/post dimensions and checking that cells outside the manipulated range retain their original values and attributes.

memory_items.title: Avoid Deprecated Pandas Methods for Fill Operations
memory_items.lesson: Use current pandas idioms for forward/backward fill operations instead of deprecated syntax.
memory_items.generalization: Replace fillna(method='ffill') with .ffill() or fillna(method='bfill') with .bfill() to avoid deprecation warnings and ensure compatibility with future pandas versions.

edits.content:
## 6. Range-Constrained Operations
- **Targeted Edits**: When a task specifies a specific cell range (e.g., "column B, rows 2-30"), use direct cell manipulation via openpyxl rather than bulk DataFrame-to-excel conversion.
- **Scope Validation**: After manipulation, verify that only cells within the specified range were modified; compare pre/post dimensions and check that cells outside the target range retain original values.
- **Preservation During Range Updates**: Use openpyxl to load the original workbook, modify cells in-place within the target range using ws[cell].value assignments, then save—avoiding to_excel() which can alter overall structure.
- **Modern API Usage**: Use current pandas methods (.ffill(), .bfill()) instead of deprecated syntax (fillna(method='ffill')).
